exercises.name: Diaphragmatic Breathing
exercises.instructions: Lie on your back, knees bent. One hand on chest, one on belly. Inhale slowly through nose — belly rises, chest stays still. Exhale slowly through pursed lips. Before exhaling, gently draw navel toward spine to activate deep stabilisers. Hold through subsequent exercises.
cues: Belly rises, chest stays still
Inhale 4 sec, exhale 6 sec
Target 6 breaths per minute

exercises.name: Seated Thoracic Rotation
exercises.instructions: Sit on edge of chair, feet flat, arms crossed on chest. Slowly rotate torso to right (5 sec), return to centre, rotate left. Movement comes from the thoracic spine, not the neck.
cues: Move from thoracic spine, not the neck
5 sec per rotation
Do not force past pain

exercises.name: Cat-Cow with Neutral Spine Hold
exercises.instructions: Start on hands and knees, wrists under shoulders, knees under hips. Cow: inhale, let belly drop, lift head and tailbone. Cat: exhale, round entire spine upward. Neutral hold: find the middle position — hold 5 seconds breathing normally.
cues: Think of each vertebra unlocking
Each cycle = cow + cat + neutral hold
Gaze at floor during neutral

exercises.name: Thoracic Extension — Towel Roll
exercises.instructions: Roll a bath towel to ~10 cm diameter. Sit in front of it, then carefully lie back so the towel is at the apex of your kyphosis (upper-mid back). Support head with hands interlaced behind neck. Let gravity gently extend thoracic spine over the roll. Move the towel one vertebra up/down for each position.
cues: Do NOT force or press down
Breathe slowly and deeply
Move towel one vertebra up each position
exercises.warning: Stop if sharp pain, dizziness, or pins and needles in arms. Never place towel under lumbar spine.

exercises.name: Bird-Dog
exercises.instructions: Quadruped position. Activate deep core. Slowly extend right arm forward (thumb up) and left leg back simultaneously. Keep hips level — no rotation. Hold 5 seconds. Return slowly. Alternate sides.
cues: Imagine a glass of water on your lower back — don't spill it
Don't lift above horizontal
Keep neck neutral — look at floor

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.instructions: Lie on back. Press lumbar spine gently to floor. Arms reach to ceiling, hips and knees at 90°. Slowly lower right arm overhead and extend left leg simultaneously — keeping low back pressed to floor. Return. Alternate sides.
cues: Low back stays glued to floor — if it lifts, stop
Breathe out as you lower the limbs
Control is everything — not range of motion

exercises.name: Prone Cobra
exercises.instructions: Lie face down, arms along your sides, palms facing ceiling. Squeeze shoulder blades together, then slowly lift chest 5-8 cm off floor. Keep neck neutral — look at floor 20 cm in front. Hold 3-5 seconds. Lower slowly.
cues: Do NOT push up with hands
Pure muscle activation only
Lift only 5-8 cm — this is not a full cobra

exercises.name: Wall Angels
exercises.instructions: Stand with back against wall, heels 10 cm from wall. Arms bent 90° at elbows, pressed against wall. Slowly slide arms up the wall to full overhead reach, then back down.
cues: Wrists AND elbows must maintain wall contact
Reduce range if you cannot keep contact
Very limited initially — this is normal if pectorals are tight

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Stretch — Kneeling
exercises.instructions: Kneel on right knee (place padding), left foot forward. Maintain tall, upright posture. Gently shift hips forward until you feel stretch at front of right hip. Do NOT lean forward. Hold 30 seconds. Switch sides.
cues: Stretch felt in front of hip/groin — not the knee
Keep pelvis neutral — no excessive lumbar arch
Upright torso throughout

exercises.name: Pectoral Doorway Stretch
exercises.instructions: Stand in a doorframe. Place forearms on the sides, elbows at 90°. Gently lean forward until you feel a stretch across the chest and front of shoulders. Hold 30 seconds.
cues: Feel the stretch across chest and front shoulders
Lean forward — do not push with arms
Breathe deeply into the stretch

exercises.name: Serratus Anterior — Wall Push-Up Plus
exercises.instructions: Stand facing wall, arms extended, palms flat at shoulder height. Elbows stay straight throughout. Push hands into wall letting shoulder blades spread apart (protraction) — hold 3 sec. Allow blades to pinch together (retraction). Repeat.
cues: Elbows stay LOCKED — no bending
Only the shoulder blades move
Feel blades push wide (protract) then pinch together (retract)
exercises.warning: Stop if shoulder blade grinding, sharp pain, or neck tension.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Mobilisation
exercises.instructions: Place foam roller horizontally on floor. Sit in front, cross arms on chest. Lean back so roller contacts lower mid-back (T7). Let weight create gentle extension. Hold 15 sec breathing slowly. Roll 2-3 cm upward to next segment. Work from T7 up to T4 — 5 positions.
cues: Do NOT roll below T7 — no lumbar contact
Do NOT roll above T4
Passive gravity — not pressing into it
exercises.warning: Stop if sharp pain, dizziness, or tingling in arms. Start with towel roll in Week 1; introduce foam roller from Week 3.

exercises.name: Child's Pose
exercises.instructions: Kneel, sit back toward heels if comfortable, arms extended forward. Hold 30-60 seconds, breathing slowly. Feel the lumbar spine gently decompress.
cues: Breathe slowly and deeply
Let spine decompress — no effort

exercises.name: Supine Knee-to-Chest
exercises.instructions: Lie on back. Gently draw both knees to chest. Rock gently side to side. Hold 30 seconds.
cues: Gentle rocking — not forced
Breathe deeply

exercises.name: Postural Reset Standing
exercises.instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart. Gently squeeze shoulder blades together. Make yourself tall — imagine a string pulling the crown of your head toward the ceiling. Hold 30 seconds breathing normally.
cues: This is the posture you are training to become automatic
Shoulder blades gently back and down
String from the crown of your head

exercises.name: Superman / Back Extension
exercises.instructions: Lie face down, arms extended overhead. Simultaneously lift arms, chest, and legs off the floor. Hold 5 seconds. Begin with just arms lifted, then just legs, then both once comfortable.
cues: Squeeze glutes during hold
Hold 5 seconds each rep
Build up: arms only → legs only → both

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.instructions: Lie on back, knees bent. Feet flat on floor, hip-width apart. Press through heels and lift hips to form a straight line from knees to shoulders. Squeeze glutes at top. Hold 3 seconds. Lower slowly.
cues: Press through HEELS — not toes
Squeeze glutes hard at top
Hold 3 seconds before lowering

exercises.name: Side-Lying Clamshell
exercises.instructions: Lie on your side, knees bent to 45°. Stack hips and shoulders. Keeping feet together, open top knee like a clamshell (30-40°). Lower slowly.
cues: Hips stay stacked — don't roll backward
Open 30-40° only
Keep feet touching throughout

exercises.name: Chin Tucks
exercises.instructions: Sit or stand tall. Without moving your entire neck, gently pull chin straight backward creating a 'double chin'. Hold 5 seconds. Release.
cues: Chin moves straight BACK — not down
Hold 5 seconds each rep
Feel a light stretch at back of neck

exercises.name: Prone Y-T-W Raises
exercises.instructions: Lie face down on mat. Y shape: arms overhead in narrow V, thumbs up. Lift both arms 5-8 cm. Hold 3 sec. Lower. T shape: arms horizontal at 90°. Lift, hold 3 sec, lower. W shape: elbows bent, forearms angled down. Lift elbows, hold 3 sec, lower.
cues: Movement is SMALL — 5-8 cm lift only
This is neuromuscular activation, not strength
Keep nose toward floor — don't lift head
exercises.warning: Progress to 0.5-1 kg only after 3 weeks of controlled form.

exercises.name: Half-Kneeling Thoracic Rotation
exercises.instructions: Kneel on left knee, right foot forward. Hands behind head or crossed on chest. Slowly rotate thoracic spine toward the front knee, leading with the shoulder. Hold 3 seconds. Breathe IN at end range — let the breath expand the rotation. Return. Switch sides.
cues: Move from RIBCAGE — not arms
Pelvis stays square — don't let hip rotate
Breathe into the rotation at end range

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.instructions: Same as glute bridge, but extend one leg straight before lifting. Drive through the planted heel and lift hips forming a straight line. Hold 5 seconds. Lower slowly.
cues: Hold 5 seconds each rep
Hips stay level — don't let one side drop
Squeeze glute of the working leg

exercises.name: Modified Side Plank
exercises.instructions: Side-lying, supported on forearm and bent knees. Lift hips to form a straight line from knees to shoulders. Hold 20-30 seconds each side. Work up to 45 seconds.
cues: Straight line from knees to shoulders
Don't let hips sag downward
Build from 20 sec to 45 sec over weeks

exercises.name: Bird-Dog with Resistance Band
exercises.instructions: Same as standard bird-dog, but hold each position 10 seconds. Add light resistance band around ankles for the leg extension. Keep hips level throughout extended hold.
cues: Hold 10 seconds each rep — this is much harder
Hips completely level — zero rotation
Light resistance band only

exercises.name: Seated Rowing with Stick
exercises.instructions: Sit tall on a chair. Hold stick with both hands shoulder-width, arms extended in front. Pull the stick toward your chest in a rowing motion while squeezing shoulder blades together hard. Slowly extend back out.
cues: Squeeze shoulder blades fully at the end of each rep
Sit tall — no slouching
Slow and controlled return

exercises.name: Single-Leg Balance — Postural Challenge
exercises.instructions: Stand on one foot, lightly touching wall or chair for safety. Maintain tall posture — shoulder blades gently retracted, gaze forward. Hold 20-30 sec without gripping. Progress 1: remove hand. Progress 2: close eyes 10-15 sec. Progress 3: hold stick overhead.
cues: Shoulder blades back and down
Gaze forward at a fixed point
Remove hand support when steady

exercises.name: Schroth Rotational Angular Breathing (RAB)
exercises.instructions: Sit tall on chair edge or cross-legged. Place one hand on the side of your ribcage that feels sunken or concave. Inhale slowly and direct breath toward your hand — expand that side outward and upward. The ribs should push your hand away. As you inhale, gently elongate spine and rotate upper back in the corrective direction. Hold 3 seconds. Exhale completely.
cues: Direct breath toward the concave (sunken) side
Ribs should push your hand away on inhale
Hold 3 seconds at full inhale
exercises.warning: If unsure of curve direction, practice tall-sitting with slow nasal diaphragmatic breathing — safe regardless of curve. Consult physiotherapist for full Schroth protocol.
